methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $114M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sage Geosystems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Cindy Taff and Lance Cook that has raised $114M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage, including KoBold Metals, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $114M total raised, implying a $217M valuation. Peer-median analysis references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals at $3.0B (Series C), Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B (Series C), and Amogy at $700M (Series C). The median peer valuation is $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B.

These methods triangulate to a range of $184M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $608M. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by stage and capital intensity variation within the climate tech peer set—reflects material uncertainty around commercialization timeline and capital requirements. The peer-median method pulls substantially higher, suggesting that if Sage's technology and market position approximate those of Series C peers, current valuation could approach $1.0B or higher. Conversely, stage-progression anchors more conservatively to capital deployed. Key uncertainties center on technology differentiation, path to revenue, and fundraising trajectory relative to peers.
